comment_text.sn12.53:1.2: The fetters are desire and lust, the “things that tighten the fetters” (saṁyojaniya dhamma) are the pleasures afforded by the senses (SN 35.122).

root_text.sn12.53:1.2: “Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.53:2.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, telañca paṭicca vaṭṭiñca paṭicca telappadīpo jhāyeyya.
root_text.sn12.53:2.2: Tatra puriso kālena kālaṁ telaṁ āsiñceyya vaṭṭiṁ upasaṁhareyya.
root_text.sn12.53:2.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, telappadīpo tadāhāro tadupādāno ciraṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ jaleyya.
root_text.sn12.53:2.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.53:3.1: Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati.
root_text.sn12.53:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, telañca paṭicca vaṭṭiñca paṭicca telappadīpo jhāyeyya.
root_text.sn12.53:4.2: Tatra puriso na kālena kālaṁ telaṁ āsiñceyya na vaṭṭiṁ upasaṁhareyya.
root_text.sn12.53:4.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, telappadīpo purimassa ca upādānassa pariyādānā aññassa ca anupahārā anāhāro nibbāyeyya.
root_text.sn12.53:4.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati.

translation_text.sn12.53:1.2: “There are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.53:1.3: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.sn12.53:1.4: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.
translation_text.sn12.53:1.5: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.
translation_text.sn12.53:1.6: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress to come to be.
translation_text.sn12.53:1.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.1: Suppose an oil lamp depended on oil and a wick to burn.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.2: And from time to time someone would pour oil in and adjust the wick.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.3: Fed and fuelled by that, the oil lamp would burn for a long time.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.4: In the same way, there are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.5: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.6: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.7: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.8: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress to come to be.
translation_text.sn12.53:2.9: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.53:3.1: There are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.53:3.3: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases.
translation_text.sn12.53:3.4: When continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases.
translation_text.sn12.53:3.5: When rebirth ceases, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress cease.
translation_text.sn12.53:3.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.53:4.1: Suppose an oil lamp depended on oil and a wick to burn.
translation_text.sn12.53:4.2: And no-one would pour oil in and adjust the wick from time to time.
translation_text.sn12.53:4.3: As the original fuel is used up and no more is added, the oil lamp would be extinguished due to not being fed.
translation_text.sn12.53:4.4: In the same way, there are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.53:4.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
